research_question: What is empirically known about data quality in European public procurement databases (TED, national platforms), and what gaps remain?
scope: Empirical research on data quality in TED (Tenders Electronic Daily) and national procurement databases. Covers field quality (placeholders, missing values), entity resolution, competition indicators (single-bidding), data standards (OCDS, eForms), and the CN-CAN gap. Focus on quantitative evidence, not legal commentary.
exclusions: pure legal analysis of procurement directives
procurement fraud detection without data quality angle

facets.name: TED Data Quality Empirics
facets.description: Studies that quantify data quality problems in TED: missing values, placeholder entries, field completeness, CN-CAN coverage gaps

search_queries.web: TED procurement data quality missing values placeholder 2020-2026

facets.name: Entity Resolution in Procurement
facets.description: Methods for matching/deduplicating buyer and supplier entities across procurement databases: record linkage, knowledge graphs, identifier systems

search_queries.web: entity resolution public procurement buyer supplier matching knowledge graph

facets.name: Single-Bidding & Competition Indicators
facets.description: Corruption Risk Index (CRI), single-bidding rates across EU, competition measurement methodology, red flags

search_queries.web: single bidding rate Europe procurement corruption risk indicator 2020-2026

facets.name: Data Standards: OCDS & eForms
facets.description: Open Contracting Data Standard adoption, eForms transition in EU, interoperability challenges, data model comparisons

search_queries.web: eForms EU procurement data standard OCDS interoperability 2024 2025 2026

facets.name: Placeholder Detection in Administrative Data
facets.description: Methods for detecting phantom/placeholder values in administrative datasets broadly (not just procurement): sentinel values, data validation, anomaly detection

search_queries.web: placeholder phantom values administrative data detection methods

suggested_leitfragen: Which empirical studies quantify data quality problems in TED specifically?
What entity resolution methods are applied to procurement data?
How has the single-bidding rate in Europe evolved over time?
What does the literature say about the CN-CAN gap (tender vs award coverage)?
What policy recommendations exist for improving procurement data quality?
Who are the most-cited researchers in this field?
How does eForms address known TED data quality issues?
